q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is urllib.request.urlopen(endpoint, ...) on line 75, which performs network I/O (HTTP request) to a user-controlled URL.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision terminated at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision terminated at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock urllib.request.urlopen to prevent actual network I/O during testing.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the URL argument passed to urlopen so the test can assert whether it is user-controlled and whether it points to an internal/disallowed address.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a fake HTTPResponse-like object (e.g., a BytesIO wrapper) containing a safe dummy body so the calling code can still read and return {'endpoint': endpoint, 'preview': body} without error.
notes: The function returns a dict with 'endpoint' and 'preview' keys, so the mock must provide a .read() method returning bytes.
Consider parameterizing the mock to return different status codes or bodies to test both success and error paths.
